Package org.eventb.ui.prettyprint

Class PrettyPrintUtils

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.ui.prettyprint.PrettyPrintUtils
public class PrettyPrintUtils extends Object
Static methods that contributors should use to ease the implementation of their contribution pretty printers.
Since:
1.3

    Display an error and log message.
    static String
    getHTMLBeginForCSSClass(String cssClass, PrettyPrintAlignments.HorizontalAlignment hAlign, PrettyPrintAlignments.VerticalAlignement vAlign)
    Method to get the HTML string that should be placed before an element is pretty printed and allowing an element to be pretty printed with a defined style and layout.

    Method to get the HTML string that should be placed after an element is pretty printed and allowing an element to be pretty printed with a defined style and layout.

    Returns a boolean value indicating is the element is set as a theorem or not.

    Converts a string input to HTML format by replacing special characters (&, <, >, space, tab).

  • Constructor Details

    • PrettyPrintUtils

      public PrettyPrintUtils()

  • Method Details

    • debugAndLogError

      public static void debugAndLogError(Exception e, String msg)
      Display an error and log message.
      Parameters:
      e - the error we catch
      msg - the error message to be displayed

    • getHTMLBeginForCSSClass

      public static String getHTMLBeginForCSSClass(String cssClass, PrettyPrintAlignments.HorizontalAlignment hAlign, PrettyPrintAlignments.VerticalAlignement vAlign)
      Method to get the HTML string that should be placed before an element is pretty printed and allowing an element to be pretty printed with a defined style and layout. The cssClass given as parameter is the corresponding CSS class that the contributor defined for an given element type. This method should be used inside the appendString() method.
      Parameters:
      cssClass - the CSS class used to pretty print the element
      hAlign - the horizontal alignment of the following pretty print
      vAlign - the vertical alignment of the following pretty print
      Returns:
      a string to be appended before the element is pretty printed

    • getHTMLEndForCSSClass

      public static String getHTMLEndForCSSClass(String cssClass, PrettyPrintAlignments.HorizontalAlignment hAlign, PrettyPrintAlignments.VerticalAlignement vAlign)
      Method to get the HTML string that should be placed after an element is pretty printed and allowing an element to be pretty printed with a defined style and layout. The cssClass given as parameter is the corresponding CSS class that the contributor defined for an given element type. This method should be used inside the appendString() method.
      Parameters:
      cssClass - the CSS class used to pretty print the element
      hAlign - the horizontal alignment of the following pretty print
      vAlign - the vertical alignment of the following pretty print
      Returns:
      a string to be appended after the element is pretty printed

    • isTheorem

      public static boolean isTheorem(IDerivedPredicateElement elem)
      Returns a boolean value indicating is the element is set as a theorem or not.
      Parameters:
      elem - the element we want to check
      Returns:
      true if the given element is set as a theorem, false otherwise

    • wrapString

      public static String wrapString(String text)
      Converts a string input to HTML format by replacing special characters (&, <, >, space, tab).
      Parameters:
      text - the text to wrap up
      Returns:
      the HTML corresponding string
